title: Bulk ePHI Export — Clarity SQL / Caboodle Extract Volume Spike

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server (2648)
spl:
index=mssql sourcetype="mssql:audit" earliest=-24h action_class="SELECT"
| where match(lower(statement),"from\s+clarity\.(pat|patient|episodes)") OR match(lower(statement),"from\s+caboodle")
| eval row_estimate=coalesce(rows,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(row_estimate) as est_rows by session_server_principal_name, _time, client_ip
| eventstats median(est_rows) as med by session_server_principal_name
| where est_rows > med*20 AND est_rows>100000
| sort - est_rows
value: Detects unusually large SELECT/BCP operations against patient fact tables that may indicate unauthorized bulk download of ePHI for exfiltration.
implementation: (1) Map ETL service accounts to allow list; (2) require data request tickets with correlation ID in SQL comment where feasible; (3) integrate DLP for flat file exports.
visualization: Time chart (rows/hour by principal), Table (top spikes).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
